meta.title: Poetry Collection — Set 2
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for the Poetry Collection — key words from 'Me!', 'The Box', and 'Sandcastle'.

questions.question: What does 'might' mean in 'It might be fun to be a fish'?
questions.explanation: 'Might' means 'maybe' or 'it is possible.' The speaker says it 'might' be fun — it could be fun, but maybe not. They are just imagining.

questions.question: 'Deep' in 'the deep, blue sea' means the sea is very far below the surface.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Deep' means very far below the surface. The deep blue sea is the part of the ocean that goes down very far.

questions.question: Dad _______ home a great big box. He carried it all the way to the door.
questions.explanation: 'Brought' is the past tense of 'bring.' Dad 'brought home the great big box' — he carried it home with him.

questions.question: In 'Me!', a fish can 'swim.' What does 'skip' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Skip' means to jump lightly, often on one foot in a playful way. In 'Me!,' the speaker notes that a fish can swim but cannot 'skip' — only people can skip!

questions.question: In 'The Box', Dad 'carved' a hole. What does 'carved' mean?
options: Cut carefully with a sharp tool
questions.explanation: 'Carved' means cut carefully, usually with a knife or sharp tool. Dad 'carved a little hole into the box' — he cut a hole to make a window or door.

questions.question: In 'Sandcastle', the sand feels soft and _______. It is wet and bendy.
questions.hint: Soft and wet; makes a squishing sound
questions.explanation: 'Squishy' means soft and wet, like wet sand or mud. 'Soft and squishy, wet and brown' describes the wet sand on the beach.

questions.question: In 'The Box', the child 'roared a dragon's roar' — this means the child made a loud, fierce sound.
questions.explanation: Yes! A 'roar' is the loud sound a fierce animal like a dragon or lion makes. The child pretended to be a dragon and made a loud roaring sound.

questions.question: What is a 'moat'?
options: A deep trench of water surrounding a castle
A tall tower of a castle
A type of sand shell found on the beach
questions.explanation: A 'moat' is a deep ditch of water that goes around a castle to protect it. In 'Sandcastle,' the children make 'a moat surrounds it all' — a water channel around their sand castle.

questions.question: Sort these poetry vocabulary words — are they VERBS (actions) or ADJECTIVES (descriptions)?
questions.explanation: 'Skip,' 'zoom,' and 'brought' are actions (verbs). 'Deep' (very far down), 'squishy' (soft and wet), and 'great' (very big) are describing words (adjectives).

questions.question: The children used shells and stones to _______ their sandcastle — to make it look pretty.
questions.hint: To make something look nice by adding pretty things
questions.explanation: 'Decorate' means to make something more beautiful by adding things. The children decorated with shells and stones.

questions.question: In 'Sandcastle', children 'scoop' the sand. What does 'scoop' mean?
options: Throw sand up in the air
Pick up sand with a curved shape like a spoon
questions.explanation: 'Scoop' means to pick up something using a curved shape — like using a spoon or bucket. The children 'scoop the sand' to put it in their bucket.

questions.question: In 'The Box', the word 'cave' and the word 'den' both mean a hiding place for an animal.
questions.explanation: A 'cave' is a natural hollow place in rock or earth. A 'den' is an animal's hidden home. Both the dragon's cave and the fox's den are hiding places — the box became each of these in the child's imagination.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a line from 'Sandcastle':
questions.explanation: Correct line: 'Scoop the sand and pack it down.' This describes how to start building a sandcastle — scoop sand and press it down firmly.

questions.question: When the poem says the box became 'a great big box,' what does 'great' mean here?
options: Very large or huge in size
questions.explanation: Here, 'great' means very large or huge. 'The great big box' — Dad brought home a very large box. 'Great' and 'big' together emphasize how huge the box was.

questions.question: Sort these words from the poems — which poem does each vocabulary word come from?
questions.explanation: 'Zoom' and 'skip' come from 'Me!' 'Cardboard' and 'dragon' come from 'The Box.' 'Moat' and 'squishy' come from 'Sandcastle.'
